lessons.title: States of Matter
objectives: Describe the three states of matter
Explain changes of state using the kinetic theory
concepts: Matter exists as solid, liquid, and gas.
Kinetic theory: Particles are in constant motion.

experiment.description: State Shifter: Adjust the temperature slider to watch particles move faster as a solid turns into a liquid and then a gas.

lessons.title: Atomic Structure
objectives: Describe the structure of the atom
Define atomic number and mass number
concepts: Atoms consist of protons, neutrons, and electrons.
Protons and neutrons are in the nucleus.

experiment.description: Atom Architect: Drag protons, neutrons, and electrons into the atom to build different elements and see their position on the Periodic Table.

lessons.title: Periodic Table Trends
objectives: Describe the arrangement of elements in the Periodic Table
Identify trends in groups and periods
concepts: Elements are arranged by increasing atomic number.

experiment.description: Trend Tracker: Click on different groups/periods to highlight trends like electronegativity or atomic radius across the Periodic Table.

lessons.title: Ionic and Covalent Bonding
objectives: Explain the formation of ionic and covalent bonds

experiment.description: Bonding Lab: Select two atoms and choose to 'transfer' or 'share' electrons to form a stable molecule or lattice.

lessons.title: Molar Mass and Calculations
concepts: The mole is a unit of measurement for amount of substance.
Molar mass is the mass of one mole of a substance.

experiment.description: Mole Machine: Input the formula of a compound and its mass to calculate the number of moles automatically, showing each step of the calculation.

lessons.title: pH Scale and Indicators
objectives: Use indicators to identify acids and bases
concepts: Acids have pH < 7, Bases have pH > 7.
Universal indicator shows a range of colors.

experiment.description: pH Tester: Dip virtual litmus paper or add drops of indicator to various household liquids (lemon juice, soap, water) to determine their pH.

lessons.title: Neutralization and Salt Formation
objectives: Identify the products of acid-base reactions

experiment.description: Titration Challenge: Carefully add a base to an acid using a burette until the indicator changes color to find the exact neutralization point.

lessons.title: Defining Redox
objectives: Define oxidation and reduction in terms of electron transfer
concepts: OIL RIG: Oxidation Is Loss, Reduction Is Gain (of electrons).

experiment.description: Electron Flow: Click on atoms in a chemical equation to 'see' the transfer of electrons and label which species is oxidized or reduced.

lessons.title: Introduction to Hydrocarbons (Alkanes)
objectives: Name and draw the first four alkanes
concepts: Hydrocarbons contain only carbon and hydrogen.

experiment.description: Carbon Chain: Drag carbon and hydrogen atoms together to build methane, ethane, propane, and butane, seeing their 3D structures.
